evals.prompt: Review this Jest test file for test smells across readability, reliability, and design dimensions
expected_patterns: sleep-based waiting OR waitForTimeout OR setTimeout
order dependency OR shared mutable state OR test isolation
over-mocking OR mock boundaries
readability smell OR obscure setup OR mystery guest
actionable feedback OR fix suggestion
anti_patterns: vague unmeasurable feedback like 'this test is bad'
flagging every smell without context OR dismissing necessary complexity
suggesting mocks for pure functions OR value objects
reviewing without running tests
focusing on coverage percentage instead of coverage quality

evals.prompt: Audit a test suite for sleep-based waiting and provide specific fixes using explicit wait conditions
expected_patterns: sleep OR waitForTimeout OR setTimeout identified as reliability smell
explicit wait condition OR polling OR event-based wait recommended
await expect OR toBeVisible OR page.getByRole
line number or code citation for the smell
explain why it matters before providing fix
anti_patterns: suggesting another sleep as a fix
rejecting sleep-based waiting without offering alternative
generic advice without framework-specific syntax

evals.prompt: Identify order dependencies in tests that pass together but fail in isolation
expected_patterns: data isolation OR fail in isolation OR different order
shared mutable state OR side effects OR own state
each test creates its own preconditions recommended
beforeEach OR test-specific setup as fix
example showing before and after
anti_patterns: accepting shared beforeAll OR shared database state
suggesting test-naming conventions as a fix for order dependency
ignoring side effects between tests

evals.prompt: Review a test file for coverage smells using the BOUNDARY framework (boundary values, null/empty, duplicates, ordering, range limits)
expected_patterns: happy path only OR missing error paths identified
boundary values OR 0, 1, max, max+1 mentioned
null OR empty OR edge case coverage gap identified
specific missing scenario described with priority P0/P1/P2 OR high/medium/low
test.each OR parameterized test recommended for boundary cases
anti_patterns: requesting coverage percentage increase without identifying missing scenarios
vague 'add more tests' without specific test cases
ignoring negative/error path coverage

evals.prompt: Detect and fix a test with multiple failure causes per test (handles creation, update, deletion in one test)
expected_patterns: multiple independent behaviors OR multiple reason to fail identified
one behavior per test recommended
test splitting OR decomposition suggested
diagnostic value OR clear failure message as rationale
one behavior per test OR SMELL OR FIX:
anti_patterns: merging separate test functions to reduce lines of code
generic assertion like toBeTruthy without explaining the issue
ignoring that failure message becomes ambiguous

evals.prompt: Analyze application code for testability issues: dependency injection, side effect isolation, pure function extraction
expected_patterns: dependency injection OR constructor injection OR service locator identified
new PostgresDB OR new PostgresDatabase OR new StripeClient OR new SendGrid
side effect isolation OR pure function extraction mentioned
interface segregation OR narrow interface suggested
HARD TO TEST OR TESTABLE
anti_patterns: focusing only on test code without analyzing application code testability
suggesting test workarounds instead of fixing the design
ignoring that testability often improves maintainability

evals.prompt: Review AI-generated test code for signature LLM failures: hallucinated locators, fabricated imports, generic test data
expected_patterns: hallucinated locator OR data-testid that doesn't exist identified
fabricated import OR plausible API that doesn't exist flagged
test@test.com OR Lorem ipsum OR John Doe OR example.com
project data factory OR project-specific fixture recommended
run test against real page to verify locator suggested
anti_patterns: accepting generic placeholder data from AI generation
assuming imports are correct without verification
ignoring project naming conventions OR fixture patterns

evals.prompt: Detect conditional test logic (if/else/switch/for loops) in test bodies and refactor to parameterized tests
expected_patterns: conditional logic OR for loop OR if/else in test identified
parameterized test OR it.each OR test.each recommended
it.each([ OR test.each
explain that conditional logic in tests is itself untested
FIX: Use parameterized OR it.each OR test.each
anti_patterns: suggesting conditional logic is acceptable if simple
recommending multiple nested describes instead of parameterization
ignoring that hidden test paths reduce debuggability

evals.prompt: Audit test assertions for weak assertion messages and diagnostic value
expected_patterns: isValid(form)).toBe(true) OR isValid
toBeTruthy OR toBeDefined OR generic assertions identified as weak
specific assertion OR detailed error object recommended
failure message that explains the problem without reading test source shown
result.errors).toEqual([]) OR result.errors
anti_patterns: accepting toBeDefined as sufficient assertion
suggesting only increasing number of assertions
ignoring that generic assertions hide failure root cause

evals.prompt: Review a test suite for giant fixtures and excessive setup per test
expected_patterns: 20+ objects OR beforeEach OR Giant Fixtures
pays the setup cost OR data it doesn't use OR every test
inline setup OR factories OR factory extraction
creates only what it needs OR each test needs
FIX: Each test creates only what OR beforeEach
anti_patterns: suggesting shared beforeAll as optimization for slow setup
ignoring that large fixtures mask which objects each test actually uses
recommending increasing database cleanup instead of reducing setup
